steps.title: Trace the passage's three-part structure
steps.content: The passage begins by establishing general scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, then narrows to one research team's specific investigation, and concludes with a key finding from their work.

steps.title: Match the structure to the correct answer
steps.content:
Choice D correctly identifies all three sequential elements of the passage: general scientific interest in shipwrecks, a specific research study, and a key finding.
The other three options all fail—A invents a “research team's reaction” never shown, B mischaracterizes the passage as explaining the Darlington's historical significance, and C fabricates construction details and overstates causal relationships.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
